codeReview.summary: I reviewed the feature metadata, mission and module guidance, worker handoff, transcript skeleton, backend-worker skill, and commit `d3b43ea48cbec2605ddf069e47ba75ede5f66273`. The implemented cleanup matches the requested behavior: `PayrollController`, `PayrollAccountingService`, `PayrollBatchPaymentRequest`, and `PayrollBatchPaymentResponse` are gone; `PayrollPaymentRequest` has been removed from the accounting DTO package and relocated to `modules/hr/dto`; `AccountingFacadeCore` no longer exposes payroll wrappers; and `openapi.json` no longer advertises `/api/v1/accounting/payroll/payments/batch`. The surviving canonical payment route at `AccountingController#recordPayrollPayment` still delegates through `AccountingFacade.recordPayrollPayment(...)`, so the accounting-host payroll payment path remains live. I did not find a high-confidence correctness regression or in-scope cleanup gap in the reviewed commit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared payroll-boundary guidance is now internally inconsistent. Mission guidance still says all journal creation must go through `AccountingFacade`, the accounting module AGENTS file says the HR payroll seam is `PayrollPostingService -> AccountingFacade`, and the HR module AGENTS file still points to `AccountingCoreEngineCore.postPayrollRun()`, while the actual code now posts payroll via `AccountingService.postPayrollRun(...)`.
The internal frontend handoff library still documents the retired batch payroll endpoint and the deleted batch DTOs, so shared contract guidance no longer matches the committed API surface.

The backend-worker skill still lacks the scoped Spotless fallback already documented in mission guidance, so resumed packets with unrelated repo-wide formatting drift appear to violate the skill even when they follow the local mission instructions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Searching /home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md for `spotlessFiles` returns no matches, while /home/realnigga/.factory/missions/cf35a73d-f4bd-4cf3-9488-fee34cf89e18/AGENTS.md:76 documents `-DspotlessFiles=...`; the feature handoff at /home/realnigga/.factory/missions/cf35a73d-f4bd-4cf3-9488-fee34cf89e18/handoffs/2026-04-05T17-46-57-058Z__foundation-payroll-dead-code-removal__84c0e8df-e2c6-4821-904e-4b4c0a40c441.json:86-101 records `followedProcedure: false` for that exact reason.

summary: PASS. The batch payroll cleanup is implemented cleanly: the dead controller/service/batch DTOs and route are removed, the canonical payroll payment endpoint remains live, and I did not find a high-confidence functional regression. I did note shared-state drift around payroll-boundary guidance, stale frontend handoff docs, and scoped Spotless fallback guidance.
